(**NY 31**) is a [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") that extends for 208.74 miles (335.93 km) across western and central [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") in the United States. The western terminus of the route is at an intersection with [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104") in the city of [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York"). Its eastern terminus is at a [traffic circle](/wiki/Traffic_circle "Traffic circle") with [NY 26](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_26 "New York State Route 26") in Vernon Center, a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") within the [town of Vernon](/wiki/Vernon_(town),_New_York "Vernon (town), New York"). Over its routing, NY 31 spans 10 counties and indirectly connects three major urban areas in [Upstate New York](/wiki/Upstate_New_York "Upstate New York"): [Buffalo](/wiki/Buffalo,_New_York "Buffalo, New York")–Niagara Falls, [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), and [Syracuse](/wiki/Syracuse,_New_York "Syracuse, New York"). The route is one of the longest routes in New York State, paralleling two similarly lengthy routes, [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104") to the north and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") to the south, as well as the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal"), as it proceeds east.

**New York State Route 31** (**NY 31**) is a [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") that extends for 208.74 miles (335.93 km) across western and central [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") in the United States. The western terminus of the route is at an intersection with [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104") in the city of [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York"). Its eastern terminus is at a [traffic circle](/wiki/Traffic_circle "Traffic circle") with [NY 26](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_26 "New York State Route 26") in Vernon Center, a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") within the [town of Vernon](/wiki/Vernon_(town),_New_York "Vernon (town), New York"). Over its routing, NY 31 spans 10 counties and indirectly connects three major urban areas in [Upstate New York](/wiki/Upstate_New_York "Upstate New York"): [Buffalo](/wiki/Buffalo,_New_York "Buffalo, New York")–Niagara Falls, [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), and [Syracuse](/wiki/Syracuse,_New_York "Syracuse, New York"). The route is one of the longest routes in New York State, paralleling two similarly lengthy routes, [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104") to the north and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") to the south, as well as the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal"), as it proceeds east.

Much of NY 31 west of [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan,_New_York "Jordan, New York") was originally designated as part of a [legislative route](/wiki/Legislative_route "Legislative route") from the late 1900s to the early 1920s. NY 31 itself was assigned in the mid-1920s, utilizing all of legislative Route 30 (modern NY 31, [NY 429](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_429 "New York State Route 429"), and NY 104) west of Rochester and much of its current alignment from Rochester to [Lenox](/wiki/Lenox,_New_York "Lenox, New York"). At Lenox, NY 31 turned southeast to follow what is now [NY 316](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_316 "New York State Route 316") and [NY 46](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_46 "New York State Route 46") to [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") in [Oneida](/wiki/Oneida,_New_York "Oneida, New York"). It was realigned by 1929 to continue west to [Lewiston](/wiki/Lewiston_(village),_New_York "Lewiston (village), New York") on [Ridge Road](/wiki/Ridge_Road_(Western_New_York) "Ridge Road (Western New York)") and altered in the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)") to continue east to [Utica](/wiki/Utica,_New_York "Utica, New York") via [Verona](/wiki/Verona,_New_York "Verona, New York"). With the advent of [U.S. Route 104](/wiki/U.S._Route_104 "U.S. Route 104") (US 104) c. 1935, NY 31 was realigned west of Rochester to follow most of its modern routing.

NY 31 was truncated westward to [NY 365](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_365 "New York State Route 365") in Verona in the early 1940s, moving the eastern terminus of NY 31 to the same junction that also had served as the northern terminus of **New York State Route 234**, a north–south route that extended southeastward to Vernon Center, since the early 1930s. The two routes continued to share a terminus until 1981, when ownership and maintenance of part of NY 234 was transferred from the state of New York to [Oneida County](/wiki/Oneida_County,_New_York "Oneida County, New York") as part of a highway maintenance swap between the two levels of government. In return, the state acquired a pair of county roads that followed a routing parallel to that of the transferred section of NY 234. The new state highways and the remainder of NY 234 became an extension of NY 31.

## Route description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Route description")]

Most of NY 31 is owned by the state of New York and maintained by the [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation") (NYSDOT); however, some sections of the route—mostly within cities—are locally owned and maintained. In the cities of [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York") and [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), NY 31 is completely city-owned.[[4]](#cite_note-inv-niagara-4)[[5]](#cite_note-inv-monroe-5) Another city-maintained section exists in the [city of Lockport](/wiki/Lockport_(city),_New_York "Lockport (city), New York") from Washburn Street (five blocks east of [NY 78](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_78 "New York State Route 78")) to the eastern city line.[[4]](#cite_note-inv-niagara-4) Two villages—[Medina](/wiki/Medina,_New_York "Medina, New York") and [Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_York "Newark, New York")—also maintain parts of NY 31. In Medina, the locally owned section begins at the [Falls Road Railroad](/wiki/Falls_Road_Railroad "Falls Road Railroad") crossing in the village center and ends at Center Street ([NY 31E](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31E "New York State Route 31E")).[[6]](#cite_note-inv-orleans-6) The portion in Newark, meanwhile, extends from Mason Street (one block west of [NY 88](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_88 "New York State Route 88")) to the eastern village line.[[7]](#cite_note-newark_quad-7)

As of 2009, the most heavily trafficked segments of NY 31 lie in and around the city of Rochester. The busiest of these, from [Interstate 590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590") (I-590) to [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65") in the eastern suburb of [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), carries in excess of 40,000 vehicles [per day on average](/wiki/Average_annual_daily_traffic "Average annual daily traffic"). Two other segments—between South and Woodbury Avenues in downtown Rochester and from NY 65 to French Road in Brighton and [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York")—have traffic volumes in excess of 30,000 vehicles. On the other hand, the portion of NY 31 in the [Oneida County](/wiki/Oneida_County,_New_York "Oneida County, New York") town of [Vernon](/wiki/Vernon_(town),_New_York "Vernon (town), New York") between the [Vernon Downs](/wiki/Vernon_Downs "Vernon Downs") racetrack and Youngs Road handles just 910 cars per day on average, making it the least-traveled segment of the route.[[1]](#cite_note-2009tvr-1)

### Western New York

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Western New York")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/West_end_of_NY_31_in_Niagara_Falls.jpg/250px-West_end_of_NY_31_in_Niagara_Falls.jpg)](/wiki/File:West_end_of_NY_31_in_Niagara_Falls.jpg)

Western terminus in Niagara Falls

NY 31 begins at an intersection with [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104") in northern Niagara Falls. After a short distance eastward through the northern fringe of the city as College Avenue, the road encounters [NY 61](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_61 "New York State Route 61") at the northeastern city line. NY 31 continues northeastward into the town of [Niagara](/wiki/Niagara,_New_York "Niagara, New York"), following Witmer Road to an interchange with [I-190](/wiki/Interstate_190_(New_York) "Interstate 190 (New York)") at exit 24. Past I-190, NY 31 meets [NY 265](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_265 "New York State Route 265") south of the [Robert Moses Niagara Power Plant](/wiki/Robert_Moses_Niagara_Power_Plant "Robert Moses Niagara Power Plant") reservoir. At the intersection, NY 31 becomes Saunders Settlement Road, a name it retains for almost 15 miles (24 km). Continuing through [Niagara County](/wiki/Niagara_County,_New_York "Niagara County, New York"), NY 31 passes the south end of the [Tuscarora Indian Reservation](/wiki/Tuscarora_Indian_Reservation "Tuscarora Indian Reservation") ahead of a brief [overlap](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") with [NY 429](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_429 "New York State Route 429"). Past the east end of the concurrency, NY 429 travels north toward NY 104 while NY 31 continues east past [SUNY Niagara](/wiki/SUNY_Niagara "SUNY Niagara") to an intersection with [NY 425](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_425 "New York State Route 425"), the final state route that NY 31 intersects before entering the [Lockport](/wiki/Lockport_(city),_New_York "Lockport (city), New York") area.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/NY_63-31_in_Medina.jpg/250px-NY_63-31_in_Medina.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_63-31_in_Medina.jpg)

NY 31 and NY 63 north through Medina village

West of Lockport, NY 31 crosses [NY 270](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_270 "New York State Route 270") and [NY 93](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_93 "New York State Route 93"). Continuing into the city, NY 31 meets [NY 78](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_78 "New York State Route 78") (Transit Road) at an intersection partially located over the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal"). NY 31 heads eastward through Lockport, intersecting the northern terminus of [NY 77](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_77 "New York State Route 77") at the eastern edge of the city. While NY 77 continues along the right-of-way of NY 31, NY 31 exits, following a northeasterly alignment through [Gasport](/wiki/Gasport,_New_York "Gasport, New York") to [Middleport](/wiki/Middleport,_New_York "Middleport, New York"), where it acts as the western and southern terminus of [NY 31E](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31E "New York State Route 31E") and [NY 271](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_271 "New York State Route 271"), respectively. Just east of town, NY 31 crosses the first of nine county lines along its routing, entering [Orleans County](/wiki/Orleans_County,_New_York "Orleans County, New York").[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

At an intersection south of [Medina](/wiki/Medina,_New_York "Medina, New York"), [NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A "New York State Route 31A") branches off, continuing straight from NY 31 while NY 31 turns north to overlap [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") for a short distance between NY 31A and NY 31E in Medina. At NY 31E, NY 31 separates from NY 63, following the path of NY 31E east out of the village. 10 miles (16 km) to the east, NY 31 crosses [NY 98](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_98 "New York State Route 98") in [Albion](/wiki/Albion_(village),_Orleans_County,_New_York "Albion (village), Orleans County, New York"). Midway between Albion and [Holley](/wiki/Holley,_New_York "Holley, New York"), NY 31 intersects the southern end of [NY 387](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_387 "New York State Route 387"). NY 31 continues to the southeast, entering Holley and intersecting [NY 237](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_237 "New York State Route 237") in the village center. The route quickly leaves Holley soon after, crossing into [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York") just over a mile (about 1.6 km) from the village line.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

### Rochester area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Rochester area")]

#### Western suburbs and downtown

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Western suburbs and downtown")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/NY_31_at_NY_31A_in_Sweden.jpg/250px-NY_31_at_NY_31A_in_Sweden.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_31_at_NY_31A_in_Sweden.jpg)

Approaching NY 31A on NY 31 westbound in Sweden

Just inside the Monroe County line, the road traverses the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") before intersecting Redman Road about 1.5 miles (2.4 km) west of the village of [Brockport](/wiki/Brockport,_New_York "Brockport, New York"). NY 31 turns south here, following Redman Road back across the Erie Canal and past the western fringe of the [SUNY Brockport](/wiki/State_University_of_New_York_at_Brockport "State University of New York at Brockport") campus to a junction with Fourth Section Road. NY 31A enters the intersection from the west and ends at NY 31 while the latter turns east onto Fourth Section Road. NY 31 progresses eastward, intersecting [NY 19](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_19 "New York State Route 19") in a commercialized area directly south of Brockport and becoming Brockport–Spencerport Road. East of the village in the surrounding town of [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden,_New_York "Sweden, New York"), NY 31 meets the southern terminus of [NY 260](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_260 "New York State Route 260"). The route continues eastward, paralleling the Erie Canal as it enters [Ogden](/wiki/Ogden,_New_York "Ogden, New York"), where NY 31 comes to the current western terminus of [NY 531](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_531 "New York State Route 531"), the Spencerport Expressway, and, shortly after, the northern terminus of [NY 36](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36 "New York State Route 36"). NY 31, now sandwiched by the canal to the north and NY 531 to the south, heads eastward into the village of [Spencerport](/wiki/Spencerport,_New_York "Spencerport, New York"), where it becomes Nichols Street and meets [NY 259](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_259 "New York State Route 259") in the center of the community.[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

Outside of Spencerport, NY 31 becomes Spencerport Road as it heads eastward into the town of [Gates](/wiki/Gates,_New_York "Gates, New York"). The route heads past residential neighborhoods to the north and mostly undeveloped land to the south as it enters the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of Elmgrove, built up around NY 31's junction with [NY 386](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_386 "New York State Route 386").[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9) Not far to the east, NY 31 enters [North Gates](/wiki/North_Gates,_New_York "North Gates, New York"), a densely populated residential community near the northern town line of Gates. In the southeastern outskirts of the community, NY 31 intersects Lyell and Howard Roads at a junction that once served as the western terminus of [NY 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47").[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)[[10]](#cite_note-1964map-10) NY 31 continues east from here as Lyell Avenue, meeting [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390") at an interchange immediately north of the route's larger interchange with [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)"). Not far to the east is the [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") city line, which NY 31 crosses upon traversing the Erie Canal once again.[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/PAETEC_Park_Exterior.jpg/250px-PAETEC_Park_Exterior.jpg)](/wiki/File:PAETEC_Park_Exterior.jpg)

Broad Street at Rochester Community Sports Complex Stadium

NY 31 follows Lyell Avenue through the city to West Broad Street, where it turns to follow Broad Street southeastward past [Rochester Community Sports Complex Stadium](/wiki/Rochester_Community_Sports_Complex_Stadium "Rochester Community Sports Complex Stadium"). It heads onward toward a pair of overpasses, one carrying I-490 and another holding the [CSX Transportation](/wiki/CSX_Transportation "CSX Transportation")-owned [Rochester Subdivision](/wiki/Rochester_Subdivision "Rochester Subdivision"). NY 31 continues under both, following the former path of the Erie Canal southeast through the city to the eastern terminus of [NY 33](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_33 "New York State Route 33") at West Main Street. NY 31 follows West Main Street to the east, passing under I-490 once again before breaking from Main Street and occupying East Broad Street. The street proceeds to the east, passing through the commercial heart of the city. Just east of Exchange Boulevard, the northern terminus of [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383"), the route crosses the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") on the [Broad Street Aqueduct](/wiki/Erie_Canal:_Second_Genesee_Aqueduct "Erie Canal: Second Genesee Aqueduct").[[9]](#cite_note-bing1-9)

East of the waterway, NY 31 follows East Broad Street to South Avenue, where it turns south for two blocks to a junction with Woodbury Boulevard. It turns east here, following Woodbury Boulevard for two blocks to Chestnut Street, at which point NY 31 turns southward to follow Chestnut Street. Westbound NY 31 in the area, however, follows one block of Woodbury Boulevard and two blocks of South Clinton Avenue to reach East Broad Street. At the [Inner Loop](/wiki/Inner_Loop_(Rochester) "Inner Loop (Rochester)"), Chestnut Street becomes Monroe Avenue, the name NY 31 retains to [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York").[[11]](#cite_note-rochester_east_quad-11) Over this stretch, NY 31 maintains a constant southeasterly progression as it passes through southeastern Rochester and the town of [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"). As the route exits downtown Rochester, it intersects I-490 at exit 18. The route continues onward, passing [Cobbs Hill Park](/wiki/Cobbs_Hill_Park "Cobbs Hill Park") and entering Brighton, where it traverses areas more commercial in nature[[12]](#cite_note-bing2-12) and meets the former routing of NY 47 a second time in an area of town known as Twelve Corners.[[13]](#cite_note-1981map-13)

#### Eastern suburbs and Wayne County

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Eastern suburbs and Wayne County")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ea/NY_31_in_Pittsford_NY_1.JPG/250px-NY_31_in_Pittsford_NY_1.JPG)](/wiki/File:NY_31_in_Pittsford_NY_1.JPG)

NY 31 in Brighton

In the eastern portion of Brighton, NY 31 meets [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590") by way of an interchange and intersects [NY 65](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_65 "New York State Route 65"). From NY 65, NY 31 continues southeast into the [town of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York"), passing along the southern fringe of [Oak Hill Country Club](/wiki/Oak_Hill_Country_Club "Oak Hill Country Club") and paralleling the former right-of-way of the [Auburn Road](/wiki/Auburn_and_Syracuse_Railroad#Auburn_road "Auburn and Syracuse Railroad") railroad line to the [village of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York"). Just inside the village limits, NY 31 crosses the Erie Canal once more and intersects the [West Shore Subdivision](/wiki/West_Shore_Subdivision "West Shore Subdivision") at-grade. In the village center, NY 31 intersects [NY 96](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_96 "New York State Route 96"). East of NY 96, the route crosses over the Erie Canal once again and exits the village. Outside of the village, NY 31 parallels I-490 for a short distance across slightly open areas before connecting to the freeway near the [Perinton](/wiki/Perinton,_New_York "Perinton, New York") town line at exit 26. The route continues east into Perinton, where it traverses the Erie Canal for one final time within the county and crosses [NY 250](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_250 "New York State Route 250") near Perinton Square Mall.[[12]](#cite_note-bing2-12)

East of the mall, the route passes through increasingly rural areas, with the exception of the hamlet of Egypt midway between NY 250 and the [Wayne County](/wiki/Wayne_County,_New_York "Wayne County, New York") line.[[12]](#cite_note-bing2-12) NY 31 progresses onward into Wayne County, where the Rochester suburbs end as the route heads eastward through the mostly rural [town of Macedon](/wiki/Macedon_(town),_New_York "Macedon (town), New York") to the [village of Macedon](/wiki/Macedon_(village),_New_York "Macedon (village), New York"). Prior to exiting the village, NY 31 meets the southern terminus of [NY 350](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_350 "New York State Route 350") and the eastern terminus of [NY 31F](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31F "New York State Route 31F"). The highway continues on, exiting the village and proceeding eastward through the town of Macedon, bordered by the Erie Canal to the north and Ganargua Creek to the south. In [Palmyra](/wiki/Palmyra_(village),_New_York "Palmyra (village), New York"), NY 31 joins [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21") through the center of the village.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)[[12]](#cite_note-bing2-12)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/NY_31_west_at_NY_89.jpg/250px-NY_31_west_at_NY_89.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_31_west_at_NY_89.jpg)

Approaching NY 89 on NY 31 westbound in [Tyre](/wiki/Tyre,_New_York "Tyre, New York")

From Palmyra east, the route parallels the Erie Canal for a considerable distance. Between Palmyra and [Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_York "Newark, New York"), NY 31 dips into [Ontario County](/wiki/Ontario_County,_New_York "Ontario County, New York") for less than a mile (about 1.6 km) before reentering Wayne County. In [Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_York "Newark, New York"), NY 31 intersects [NY 88](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_88 "New York State Route 88"). Farther east, in [Lyons](/wiki/Lyons_(hamlet),_New_York "Lyons (hamlet), New York"), it crosses [NY 14](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_14 "New York State Route 14") and the Erie Canal. At [Clyde](/wiki/Clyde,_New_York "Clyde, New York"), NY 31 briefly overlaps [NY 414](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_414 "New York State Route 414") in the center of the village. Outside of Clyde, the Erie Canal veers off to the southeast while NY 31 continues east alongside the Rochester Subdivision to the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of [Savannah](/wiki/Savannah,_New_York "Savannah, New York"), the final centralized community within Wayne County. Here, NY 31 intersects [NY 89](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_89 "New York State Route 89") and follows it southward toward the [Seneca County](/wiki/Seneca_County,_New_York "Seneca County, New York") line. At the county line, NY 89 turns west to follow the county line while NY 31 heads southeast into Seneca County. The route proceeds across the northeasternmost corner of the county before crossing into [Cayuga County](/wiki/Cayuga_County,_New_York "Cayuga County, New York") via a bridge over the Erie Canal after just 1.5 miles (2.4 km).[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)[[12]](#cite_note-bing2-12)

### Central New York

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Central New York")]

Shortly after entering Cayuga County, NY 31 crosses and begins to parallel the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")), which NY 31 does for most of its routing west of the outer [Syracuse](/wiki/Syracuse,_New_York "Syracuse, New York") suburbs. In [Port Byron](/wiki/Port_Byron,_New_York "Port Byron, New York"), NY 31 intersects [NY 38](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_38 "New York State Route 38") in the center of the village. The routes embark on an overlap through the village before separating near the eastern edge of Port Byron. NY 31 continues alone to [Weedsport](/wiki/Weedsport,_New_York "Weedsport, New York"), where the route meets [NY 34](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_34 "New York State Route 34") and [County Route 31B](/wiki/County_Route_31B_(Cayuga_County,_New_York) "County Route 31B (Cayuga County, New York)") (CR 31B, formerly [NY 31B](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31B "New York State Route 31B")) north of the village. East of Weedsport, NY 31 and the Thruway are joined by the CSX Rochester Subdivision mainline, which runs down the center of the strip of land created by the Thruway to the north and NY 31 to the south.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7a/Junction_of_NY_38_with_NY_31.jpg/250px-Junction_of_NY_38_with_NY_31.jpg)](/wiki/File:Junction_of_NY_38_with_NY_31.jpg)

Western terminus of the NY 31 / NY 38 overlap in Port Byron

Continuing into [Onondaga County](/wiki/Onondaga_County,_New_York "Onondaga County, New York") and the village of [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan,_New_York "Jordan, New York"), NY 31 begins to make a turn northward while connecting to the northern end of [NY 317](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") (the former northern terminus of [NY 31C](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31C "New York State Route 31C")) a short distance north of the village center. With the turn complete, NY 31 traverses the CSX mainline and the Thruway via successive overpasses before returning to level ground and resuming an eastward alignment in the shadow of the Thruway. Once inside the limits of [Van Buren](/wiki/Van_Buren,_New_York "Van Buren, New York"), NY 31 separates from the Thruway, curving north and intersecting the western terminus of [NY 173](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_173 "New York State Route 173") in quick succession. The alignment remains fairly straight to the outskirts of [Baldwinsville](/wiki/Baldwinsville,_New_York "Baldwinsville, New York"), where NY 31 turns to the right onto Downer Street to avoid the [Seneca River](/wiki/Seneca_River_(New_York) "Seneca River (New York)") to the immediate north. The eastward progression is limited, however, as NY 31 merges with [NY 690](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_690 "New York State Route 690") northward, traversing the Seneca River and straddling the western edge of Baldwinsville.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)

On the north bank of the river, NY 31 exits NY 690 but joins [NY 370](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_370 "New York State Route 370") at the end of the exit ramp. Together, NY 31 and 370 enter Baldwinsville, intersecting [NY 48](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_48 "New York State Route 48") in the village center. Three blocks from NY 48, NY 370 splits from NY 31. As NY 370 heads for downtown [Syracuse](/wiki/Syracuse,_New_York "Syracuse, New York"), NY 31 continues east, overlapping [NY 631](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_631 "New York State Route 631") for a short distance before passing through [Lysander New Community](/wiki/Lysander_New_Community,_New_York "Lysander New Community, New York") and crossing the Seneca River again. Now in the town of [Clay](/wiki/Clay,_New_York "Clay, New York"), NY 31 meets [NY 481](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_481 "New York State Route 481") at an interchange in the shadow of the [Great Northern Mall](/wiki/Great_Northern_Mall_(New_York) "Great Northern Mall (New York)"). NY 31 continues eastward through the town to [Cicero](/wiki/Cicero,_New_York "Cicero, New York"), where it meets [US 11](/wiki/U.S._Route_11_in_New_York "U.S. Route 11 in New York") and [I-81](/wiki/Interstate_81_in_New_York "Interstate 81 in New York") at exit 30. As NY 31 heads away from I-81, [Oneida Lake](/wiki/Oneida_Lake "Oneida Lake") slowly becomes visible to the north. NY 31 comes within a mile (about 1.6 km) of the lakeshore before turning slightly to parallel the shoreline. Near the [Madison County](/wiki/Madison_County,_New_York "Madison County, New York") line, here delimited by [Chittenango Creek](/wiki/Chittenango_Creek "Chittenango Creek"), NY 31 enters the western half of [Bridgeport](/wiki/Bridgeport,_New_York "Bridgeport, New York") and meets the eastern terminus of [NY 298](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_298 "New York State Route 298") at a roundabout near the banks of the creek. NY 31 continues eastward, crossing the creek and entering Madison County and the remaining portion of Bridgeport.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/NY_31_east_at_NY_46.jpg/250px-NY_31_east_at_NY_46.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_31_east_at_NY_46.jpg)

Eastbound NY 31 at NY 46 in Verona

East of Bridgeport, NY 31 moves closer to Oneida Lake, eventually coming to within 200 yards (180 m) of the lakeshore at points. Near the eastern edge of Oneida Lake, NY 31 intersects [NY 13](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_13 "New York State Route 13") at a roundabout in the hamlet of [South Bay](/wiki/South_Bay,_New_York "South Bay, New York"). To the east, NY 31 meets the northern terminus of [NY 316](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_316 "New York State Route 316") at a junction adjacent to the [Oneida County](/wiki/Oneida_County,_New_York "Oneida County, New York") line, again marked by a body of water, the [Oneida Creek](/wiki/Oneida_Creek "Oneida Creek"). NY 31 traverses the creek and proceeds eastward through the Oneida County town of [Verona](/wiki/Verona,_New_York "Verona, New York"). Northeast of [Oneida](/wiki/Oneida,_New_York "Oneida, New York"), NY 31 intersects [NY 46](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_46 "New York State Route 46") adjacent to the path of the old routing of the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal"). Farther east, NY 31 intersects [NY 365](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_365 "New York State Route 365") just north of its interchange with the Thruway. The [Turning Stone Resort & Casino](/wiki/Turning_Stone_Resort_%26_Casino "Turning Stone Resort & Casino"), located 2 miles (3 km) south of the intersection, is accessible via NY 365.[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)

Past NY 365, NY 31 passes over the Thruway (I-90) for the final time. The route continues southeast into the [town of Vernon](/wiki/Vernon_(town),_New_York "Vernon (town), New York") and the [village of the same name](/wiki/Vernon_(village),_New_York "Vernon (village), New York") within, where NY 31 intersects [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5"). NY 31 joins NY 5, following the route eastward for about 0.75 miles (1.21 km) out of the village to Stuhlman Road. Here, NY 31 splits from NY 5 and follows Stuhlman Road southward, passing to the east of [Vernon Downs](/wiki/Vernon_Downs "Vernon Downs") before intersecting Youngs Road. Stuhlman Road ends here; however, NY 31 turns eastward onto Youngs Road, following it into the hamlet of Vernon Center. The route continues eastward into the center of the community, where it ends at a large [traffic circle](/wiki/Traffic_circle "Traffic circle") with [NY 26](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_26 "New York State Route 26").[[8]](#cite_note-ilnymap-8)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: History")]

### Early designations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Early designations")]

In 1908, the [New York State Legislature](/wiki/New_York_State_Legislature "New York State Legislature") established a statewide [legislative route](/wiki/Legislative_route "Legislative route") system that initially consisted of 37 [unsigned](/wiki/Unsigned_highway "Unsigned highway") routes. Much of what is now NY 31 west of [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan,_New_York "Jordan, New York") became part of one of three routes, namely Route 14, Route 20, and Route 30. Route 30 began at the [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York") city line (delimited by modern [NY 61](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_61 "New York State Route 61")) and followed current NY 31 east to [Sanborn](/wiki/Sanborn,_New_York "Sanborn, New York"), where it turned north onto modern [NY 429](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_429 "New York State Route 429"). The route followed current NY 429 to [Ridge Road](/wiki/Ridge_Road_(Western_New_York) "Ridge Road (Western New York)") in [Porter](/wiki/Porter,_New_York "Porter, New York"), at which point it turned to follow Ridge Road eastward to what is now [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") in [Ridgeway](/wiki/Ridgeway,_New_York "Ridgeway, New York"). Route 30 went south here, utilizing modern NY 63 between Ridgeway and Medina. It rejoined what is now NY 31 in the latter location and proceeded eastward on roughly current NY 31 to the [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") city line. In the vicinity of [Brockport](/wiki/Brockport,_New_York "Brockport, New York"), however, Route 30 was routed on West Avenue and modern [NY 19](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_19 "New York State Route 19") instead. From the southeastern city line to [NY 96](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_96 "New York State Route 96") in the [village of Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York"), Monroe Avenue (modern NY 31) was part of Route 14, which turned south onto South Main Street in the center of Pittsford. Lastly, the segment what is now NY 31 between current [NY 31F](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31F "New York State Route 31F") and [NY 350](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_350 "New York State Route 350") in the [village of Macedon](/wiki/Macedon_(village),_New_York "Macedon (village), New York") and [NY 317](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") in Jordan was part of Route 20.[[14]](#cite_note-1909book-14)[[15]](#cite_note-1920book-15)

Two spur routes were added to the definition of Route 30 in 1914.[[16]](#cite_note-1919book-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) One of these, a route connecting [Lockport](/wiki/Lockport_(city),_New_York "Lockport (city), New York") to Medina, utilized what is now NY 31 from the Lockport city line to [NY 77](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_77 "New York State Route 77") and from Gasport Road in [Gasport](/wiki/Gasport,_New_York "Gasport, New York") to Medina (via modern [NY 31E](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31E "New York State Route 31E")).[[15]](#cite_note-1920book-15)[[16]](#cite_note-1919book-16) On March 1, 1921, several routes were altered or eliminated as part of a partial renumbering of the legislative route system. Among these was Route 30, which was modified to follow Ridge Road instead between Ridgeway and Rochester. Its former routing from Medina eastward and the Lockport spur of Route 30 mostly became part of an extended Route 20, which now began in [North Tonawanda](/wiki/North_Tonawanda,_New_York "North Tonawanda, New York") and followed modern NY 425 and Lower Mountain, Gothic Hill, and Upper Mountain Roads north and east through Cambria Center to Lockport. From [Middleport](/wiki/Middleport,_New_York "Middleport, New York") to Medina, however, Route 20 was routed on modern NY 31 instead. East of Rochester, the section of Route 20 between [Fairport](/wiki/Fairport,_New_York "Fairport, New York") and Macedon was reconfigured to utilize what is now [NY 250](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_250 "New York State Route 250") and NY 31.[[18]](#cite_note-1921book-18)

### Assignment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Assignment")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/NY_5_east_at_NY_46.jpg/250px-NY_5_east_at_NY_46.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_5_east_at_NY_46.jpg)

The junction of NY 5 and NY 46 in Oneida (pictured in May 2005) was NY 31's eastern terminus during the 1920s.

The first set of posted routes in New York were assigned in 1924. At this time, all of legislative Route 20 west of Rochester became part of [NY 3](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_3 "New York State Route 3"), a cross-state route that began in North Tonawanda and ended in [Plattsburgh](/wiki/Plattsburgh_(city),_New_York "Plattsburgh (city), New York").[[2]](#cite_note-1924nyt-2)[[3]](#cite_note-1926map-3) By 1926, NY 31 was assigned to an alignment extending from Niagara Falls to [Oneida](/wiki/Oneida,_New_York "Oneida, New York"). The route began at [NY 34](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_34_(1924%E2%80%931930) "New York State Route 34 (1924–1930)") (now [NY 104](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_104 "New York State Route 104")) in Niagara Falls and followed College Avenue east to the city line, from where it continued eastward to Rochester on the alignment of legislative Route 30.[[3]](#cite_note-1926map-3) Within the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), NY 31 continued east on Ridge Road to Lake Avenue, where it turned south to enter downtown Rochester, becoming State Street at Lyell Avenue. At East Main Street, NY 31 resumed its eastward alignment, crossing the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") and continuing on Main Street to Monroe Avenue, which, at the time, connected directly with Main Street. NY 31 turned right on Monroe Avenue, rejoining its present alignment of NY 31 near Union Street.[[19]](#cite_note-gb-19)

East of Rochester, NY 31 continued eastward to Jordan, utilizing the Rochester–Pittsford portion of legislative Route 14, the [Perinton](/wiki/Perinton,_New_York "Perinton, New York")–Jordan segment of Route 20, and its current alignment between Pittsford and Perinton. From [Weedsport](/wiki/Weedsport,_New_York "Weedsport, New York") to Jordan, NY 31 used a slightly different routing. It initially exited the village on Clinton Road and followed that road to Jordan, where NY 31 turned north and followed modern [NY 317](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") and NY 31 north and east to modern CR 84. NY 31 turned north here, using what is now Old Route 31, current NY 31, and Downer Street to reach [Baldwinsville](/wiki/Baldwinsville,_New_York "Baldwinsville, New York"). Within the village, NY 31 followed Downer Street and what is now [NY 48](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_48 "New York State Route 48") along Syracuse Street between Downer and Genesee Streets. Outside of Baldwinsville, NY 31 remained on its current routing to [Cicero](/wiki/Cicero,_New_York "Cicero, New York"), where it followed a more northerly alignment along Lakeshore Road. It rejoined its modern alignment near the eastern town line, and followed it east to what is now [NY 316](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_316 "New York State Route 316") in [Lenox](/wiki/Lenox,_New_York "Lenox, New York"). From here, NY 31 went southeastward to [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") in Oneida by way of modern NY 316 and [NY 46](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_46 "New York State Route 46").[[3]](#cite_note-1926map-3)

### Truncations and extensions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Truncations and extensions")]

In the late 1920s, NY 31 was rerouted west of what is now NY 429 to follow Ridge Road to [Lewiston](/wiki/Lewiston_(village),_New_York "Lewiston (village), New York"), where it ended at then-NY 34. The former routing of NY 31 into downtown Niagara Falls was redesignated as [NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A_(western_Niagara_County) "New York State Route 31A (western Niagara County)").[[20]](#cite_note-1927map-20)[[21]](#cite_note-1929map-21) In the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)"), NY 31 was realigned east of Oneida Valley to continue east to [Utica](/wiki/Utica,_New_York "Utica, New York") by way of its modern alignment plus Spring and Lowell Roads and several city streets. At the same time, NY 3 was realigned to continue west to Niagara Falls over modern NY 31, [overlapping](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") NY 31A west of Sanborn. Two other portions of NY 3—from the modern junction of NY 31 and [NY 77](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_77 "New York State Route 77") east of Lockport to Gasport and between Middleport and Medina—were also realigned at this time to use modern NY 31 and the old routing of legislative Route 30 Spur along modern NY 31E, respectively.[[19]](#cite_note-gb-19)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6e/NY_31_east_end_at_NY_26.jpg/250px-NY_31_east_end_at_NY_26.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_31_east_end_at_NY_26.jpg)

Sign assembly at NY 31's eastern terminus in Vernon. The junction with NY 26 was NY 234's southern terminus from the early 1930s to 1981.

What is now NY 31 from [NY 365](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_365 "New York State Route 365") in [Verona](/wiki/Verona,_New_York "Verona, New York") to NY 5 in [Vernon](/wiki/Vernon_(village),_New_York "Vernon (village), New York") became part of NY 234 c. 1932. The new north–south highway continued south to [NY 26](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_26 "New York State Route 26") in Vernon Center by way of NY 5 and Churton and Youngs Roads. Also around this time, NY 3 was realigned between Shawnee and Lockport to use Saunders Settlement Road.[[24]](#cite_note-1931map-24)[[25]](#cite_note-1932map-25) [US 104](/wiki/U.S._Route_104 "U.S. Route 104"), meanwhile, was assigned c. 1935, following Ridge Road from Niagara Falls east to [Maple View](/wiki/Maple_View,_New_York "Maple View, New York") in [Oswego County](/wiki/Oswego_County,_New_York "Oswego County, New York"). The assignment of the highway led to the reconfiguration of several state highways in [western](/wiki/Western_New_York "Western New York") and [central New York](/wiki/Central_New_York "Central New York"), including NY 3 and NY 31. The latter route was rerouted west of Rochester to follow the former alignment of NY 3 to Niagara Falls while NY 3 was truncated to a location in northern [Cayuga County](/wiki/Cayuga_County,_New_York "Cayuga County, New York"). At the same time, NY 31A was reassigned to [a new alignment](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A "New York State Route 31A") extending from Middleport to Knowlesville via Medina.[[26]](#cite_note-1934map-26)[[27]](#cite_note-1935map-27)

NY 31 was truncated to NY 365 in Verona in the early 1940s; as a result, NY 31 and NY 234 now had a common terminus.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29) Despite this fact, the two routes continued to co-exist for the next 40 years.[[30]](#cite_note-2009log-30)[[31]](#cite_note-1977map-31) On April 1, 1981, ownership and maintenance of the Churton Road portion of NY 234 was transferred from the state of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") to [Oneida County](/wiki/Oneida_County,_New_York "Oneida County, New York") as part of a highway maintenance swap between the two levels of government. Two of highways that the state received in return were Stuhlman Road (CR 81) and the segment of Youngs Road ([CR 13](/wiki/County_Route_13_(Oneida_County,_New_York) "County Route 13 (Oneida County, New York)")) between Stuhlman Road and Churton Road (NY 234).[[32]](#cite_note-vernon_quad-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) The two new state highways did not become a rerouting of NY 234, however, as the NY 234 designation was eliminated at this time.[[30]](#cite_note-2009log-30) Instead, NY 31 was extended southeastward over NY 234's former routing to Vernon, from where it continued to Vernon Center by way of NY 5, Stuhlman and Youngs Roads.[[34]](#cite_note-1985map-34) Churton Road was redesignated as CR 94.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

### Realignments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Realignments")]

Several small-scale realignments of NY 31 have occurred over the years. NY 31 was altered c. 1938 to follow its modern alignment through Cicero, bypassing Lakeshore Road to the south.[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-1938map-37) In the late 1930s, NY 31 was moved onto a new, more northern highway between Weedsport and Jordan.[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[39]](#cite_note-39) The section of the route between Middleport and Medina was shifted southward on January 1, 1949, to follow what had been NY 31A between the two villages. NY 31's old northerly routing became [NY 31E](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31E "New York State Route 31E").[[40]](#cite_note-1948change-40) The portion of NY 31 northeast of Jordan was straightened at some point in the late 1970s or early 1980s to bypass the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of Jacks Reef to the south and east.[[13]](#cite_note-1981map-13)[[41]](#cite_note-41) In the early 1980s, NY 31 was rerouted near Baldwinsville to follow [NY 690](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_690 "New York State Route 690") and [NY 370](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_370 "New York State Route 370") into the village.[[13]](#cite_note-1981map-13)[[34]](#cite_note-1985map-34)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c9/NY_31_east_at_Redman_Road.jpg/250px-NY_31_east_at_Redman_Road.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_31_east_at_Redman_Road.jpg)

NY 31 east at Redman Road in Sweden. Until the early 1980s, NY 31 continued straight at this junction to serve the village of Brockport.

Around the same time as the Baldwinsville realignment, NY 31 was moved onto its current alignment southwest of Brockport, bypassing the village on Redman and Fourth Section Roads and eliminating an overlap with [NY 19](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_19 "New York State Route 19"). The new alignment of NY 31 utilized part of [NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A "New York State Route 31A"), which was truncated westward to the junction of Redman and Fourth Section Roads.[[13]](#cite_note-1981map-13)[[34]](#cite_note-1985map-34) [Reference markers](/wiki/Reference_marker_(New_York) "Reference marker (New York)") along the Fourth Section Road segment still read "31A".[[1]](#cite_note-2009tvr-1) The former routing of NY 31 into Brockport became [NY 943B](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_943B "New York State Route 943B"), an unsigned [reference route](/wiki/Reference_route_(New_York) "Reference route (New York)"), and later also became part of [NY 19 Truck](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_19_Truck_(Brockport) "New York State Route 19 Truck (Brockport)").[[42]](#cite_note-42) The Redman Road portion of NY 31, meanwhile, was initially maintained by [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York") as part of [CR 236](/wiki/County_Route_236_(Monroe_County,_New_York) "County Route 236 (Monroe County, New York)").[[43]](#cite_note-brockport_quad-43) In 2007, ownership and maintenance of the Redman Road segment of NY 31 was transferred from Monroe County to the state of New York as part of a highway maintenance swap between the two levels of government. A bill (S4856, 2007) to enact the swap was introduced in the [New York State Senate](/wiki/New_York_State_Senate "New York State Senate") on April 23 and passed by both the Senate and the [New York State Assembly](/wiki/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") on June 20. The act was signed into law by Governor [Eliot Spitzer](/wiki/Eliot_Spitzer "Eliot Spitzer") on August 28. Under the terms of the act, it took effect 90 days after it was signed into law; thus, the maintenance swap officially took place on November 26, 2007.[[44]](#cite_note-law-44)

### Rochester

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Rochester")]

The portion of NY 31 in downtown Rochester between Brown Street and South Avenue runs along the old path of the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") through the city. When the canal was diverted to bypass the city to the south in 1920, the canal bed became part of the [Rochester Subway](/wiki/Rochester_Subway "Rochester Subway") while a 4,000-foot (1,219 m) bridge was built over the bed to carry a new street named Broad Street.[[45]](#cite_note-overhaul-45) None of Broad Street was initially part of NY 31, however; when NY 31 replaced NY 3 west of Rochester c. 1935, it entered the city by way of NY 3's old routing on Lyell Avenue and rejoined its original alignment at State Street.[[27]](#cite_note-1935map-27) In the late 1930s, NY 31 was realigned through downtown to follow Smith and Bausch Streets east across the Genesee River and St. Paul Street and South Avenue south to Woodbury Boulevard, which at the time connected to Monroe Avenue one block to the east at South Clinton Avenue.[[37]](#cite_note-1938map-37)[[46]](#cite_note-46) NY 31 was altered once again c. 1964 to leave Lyell Avenue at Broad Street and follow West and East Broad Streets through downtown to South Avenue.[[10]](#cite_note-1964map-10)[[47]](#cite_note-1963map-47)

From West Main Street to South Avenue, the bridge supports were largely built with concrete while the supports on the section between Brown Street and West Main Street were mostly built with steel. Unlike the concrete supports, the steel supports flexed throughout the year, creating holes and gaps in the pavement on the deck above.[[48]](#cite_note-gaps-48) The city of Rochester, which owns and maintains the section of NY 31 within the city,[[5]](#cite_note-inv-monroe-5) closed the outer northbound lane of West Broad Street (westbound NY 31) between Brown and Main Streets in June 2008 for this reason.[[48]](#cite_note-gaps-48)

In February 2010, work began on a $17.5 million project to either demolish or rehabilitate the Broad Street bridge between Brown Street and Exchange Boulevard ([NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383")).[[49]](#cite_note-restrict-49) As part of the project, the section of the bridge between Brown and West Main Streets would be removed—allowing for the canal bed to be filled in and the road to be rebuilt—while the segment between West Main Street and Exchange Boulevard would be rehabilitated.[[45]](#cite_note-overhaul-45) The portion of Broad Street between Allen and Main Streets was closed to all traffic in February[[49]](#cite_note-restrict-49) and was originally scheduled to open on October 30.[[45]](#cite_note-overhaul-45) It finally reopened to traffic on December 3.[[50]](#cite_note-reopen-50)

Farther north, work on the segment between Brown and Allen Streets was performed on one side at a time, keeping one side of Broad Street open at all times. The northbound lanes of this segment were closed on April 7,[[45]](#cite_note-overhaul-45) and one direction of the segment was closed until November.[[49]](#cite_note-restrict-49) It fully reopened on December 3 along with the West Main Street–Allen Street segment. Rehabilitation of Broad Street from West Main Street to Exchange Boulevard was expected to be completed in mid-to-late 2011.[[50]](#cite_note-reopen-50) The entire project, which also calls for landscape improvements to the area surrounding West Broad Street, was tentatively scheduled to be finished in February 2012.[[45]](#cite_note-overhaul-45)

### Spencerport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Spencerport")]

On June 8, 2017, [Governor of New York](/wiki/Governor_of_New_York "Governor of New York") [Andrew Cuomo](/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") announced that they would upgrade the intersection of NY 531 and NY 36. This would improve the connection between NY 531 and NY 31 for safety purposes. Instead of the abrupt end at NY 36, a new road would be constructed to connect NY 531 and NY 31. NY 31 would be widened from Gallup Road to NY 531; a center median would be added and a portion of NY 31 between NY 531 and NY 36 would become a cul-de-sac for the residences in the area. NYSDOT projects that the job would be completed by the end of 2018.[[51]](#cite_note-531-31connection-51)

## Suffixed routes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_31&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Suffixed routes")]

NY 31 has had as many as eight suffixed routes using six different designations; all but three have been eliminated or renumbered.

* The NY 31A designation has been used for three distinct highways, two of which briefly co-existed:
  + The [first NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") was a connector between NY 31 in [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan,_New_York "Jordan, New York") and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") in the [village of Elbridge](/wiki/Elbridge_(village),_New_York "Elbridge (village), New York"). It was assigned in the mid-1920s[[2]](#cite_note-1924nyt-2)[[3]](#cite_note-1926map-3) and absorbed by [NY 41](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_41 "New York State Route 41") in the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)").[[19]](#cite_note-gb-19)
  + The [second NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_429 "New York State Route 429") was an alternate route of NY 31 between [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York") and [Cambria](/wiki/Cambria,_New_York "Cambria, New York") via [Sanborn](/wiki/Sanborn,_New_York "Sanborn, New York"). It was assigned in the late 1920s[[20]](#cite_note-1927map-20)[[21]](#cite_note-1929map-21) and mostly supplanted by a realigned NY 31 c. 1935.[[26]](#cite_note-1934map-26)[[27]](#cite_note-1935map-27)
  + The [current NY 31A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A "New York State Route 31A") (22.89 miles or 36.84 kilometres) is an alternate route of NY 31 between the villages of [Medina](/wiki/Medina,_New_York "Medina, New York") and [Brockport](/wiki/Brockport,_New_York "Brockport, New York"). The route bypasses Medina, [Albion](/wiki/Albion_(village),_New_York "Albion (village), New York"), and [Holley](/wiki/Holley,_New_York "Holley, New York") to the south via [Clarendon](/wiki/Clarendon,_New_York "Clarendon, New York").[[1]](#cite_note-2009tvr-1) It was assigned c. 1935.[[26]](#cite_note-1934map-26)[[27]](#cite_note-1935map-27)
* The NY 31B designation has been used for two distinct highways:
  + The first NY 31B was a short-lived connector in [Cicero](/wiki/Cicero,_New_York "Cicero, New York") that linked Lakeshore Road (at the time NY 31) to [US 11](/wiki/U.S._Route_11_in_New_York "U.S. Route 11 in New York") by way of Mud Mill Road. It was assigned c. 1932[[24]](#cite_note-1931map-24)[[25]](#cite_note-1932map-25) and removed c. 1933.[[25]](#cite_note-1932map-25)[[52]](#cite_note-1933map-52)
  + The [second NY 31B](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31B "New York State Route 31B") was a connector linking NY 31 in [Weedsport](/wiki/Weedsport,_New_York "Weedsport, New York") to NY 5 in the [town of Elbridge](/wiki/Elbridge_(town),_New_York "Elbridge (town), New York").[[31]](#cite_note-1977map-31) The designation was assigned c. 1933[[25]](#cite_note-1932map-25)[[52]](#cite_note-1933map-52) and removed in 1980.[[30]](#cite_note-2009log-30) It was replaced with [CR 31B](/wiki/County_Route_31B_(Cayuga_County,_New_York) "County Route 31B (Cayuga County, New York)") in Cayuga County and [CR 99](/wiki/County_Route_99_(Onondaga_County,_New_York) "County Route 99 (Onondaga County, New York)") in Onondaga County.[[53]](#cite_note-inv-cayuga-53)[[54]](#cite_note-jordan_quad-54)
* [NY 31C](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") was a connector between NY 31 in Jordan to NY 5 in Elbridge.[[55]](#cite_note-55) It was assigned c. 1933[[25]](#cite_note-1932map-25)[[52]](#cite_note-1933map-52) and partially replaced by [NY 317](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_317 "New York State Route 317") in 2003.[[30]](#cite_note-2009log-30)
* [NY 31D](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31A "New York State Route 31A") was a spur that extended from the [Orleans County](/wiki/Orleans_County,_New_York "Orleans County, New York") line to NY 31 south of Brockport in Monroe County. The route was assigned c. 1935[[26]](#cite_note-1934map-26)[[27]](#cite_note-1935map-27) and became the easternmost portion of NY 31A c. 1963.[[47]](#cite_note-1963map-47)[[56]](#cite_note-56)
* [NY 31E](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31E "New York State Route 31E") (5.29 miles or 8.51 kilometres) is an alternate route of NY 31 between the villages of [Middleport](/wiki/Middleport,_New_York "Middleport, New York") and Medina.[[1]](#cite_note-2009tvr-1) It was assigned in 1949.[[40]](#cite_note-1948change-40)
* [NY 31F](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31F "New York State Route 31F") (13.59 miles or 21.87 kilometres) is an alternate route of NY 31 that extends from [NY 96](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_96 "New York State Route 96") in [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(town),_New_York "Pittsford (town), New York") to NY 31 in [Macedon](/wiki/Macedon_(village),_New_York "Macedon (village), New York"). The route bypasses [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York") and southern [Perinton](/wiki/Perinton,_New_York "Perinton, New York") to serve [East Rochester](/wiki/East_Rochester,_New_York "East Rochester, New York") and [Fairport](/wiki/Fairport,_New_York "Fairport, New York").[[1]](#cite_note-2009tvr-1) It was assigned in 1949.[[40]](#cite_note-1948change-40)
